description: Dependency graph showing how the first five propositions of Euclid's Elements depend on postulates (P1–P3), common notions (CN1, CN3, CN4, CN5), and each other. Demonstrates the axiomatic structure of Euclidean geometry: postulates (red), common notions (purple), and propositions (teal) with their logical dependencies.
mermaid:
graph TD
    P1["Post. 1\nDraw a straight line\nbetween two points"]
    P2["Post. 2\nExtend a straight line\ncontinuously"]
    P3["Post. 3\nDraw a circle with given\ncenter and radius"]
    CN1["CN 1\nThings equal to the same\nthing are equal to each other"]
    CN3["CN 3\nIf equals subtracted from\nequals, remainders are equal"]
    CN4["CN 4\nThings coinciding with\none another are equal"]
    CN5["CN 5\nThe whole is greater\nthan the part"]
    Prop1["Prop. I.1\nConstruct an equilateral\ntriangle on a given line"]
    Prop2["Prop. I.2\nPlace a line segment equal\nto a given segment at a point"]
    Prop3["Prop. I.3\nCut off from the greater of\ntwo lines a segment equal to the less"]
    Prop4["Prop. I.4\nSAS Congruence:\ntwo triangles with two equal sides\nand included angle are congruent"]
    Prop5["Prop. I.5\nBase angles of an\nisosceles triangle are equal"]
    P1 --> Prop1
    P3 --> Prop1
    Prop1 --> Prop2
    P1 --> Prop2
    P2 --> Prop2
    P3 --> Prop2
    Prop2 --> Prop3
    P3 --> Prop3
    CN4 --> Prop4
    CN5 --> Prop4
    Prop1 --> Prop5
    P1 --> Prop5
    P2 --> Prop5
    CN1 --> Prop5
    CN3 --> Prop5
    Prop4 --> Prop5
    classDef postulate fill:#e74c3c,color:#fff,stroke:#c0392b
    classDef commonnotion fill:#9b59b6,color:#fff,stroke:#8e44ad
    classDef proposition fill:#1abc9c,color:#fff,stroke:#16a085
    class P1,P2,P3 postulate
    class CN1,CN3,CN4,CN5 commonnotion
    class Prop1,Prop2,Prop3,Prop4,Prop5 proposition
name: Euclid's Elements Book I — Propositions 1–5
notes: Dependency graph of Euclid's Elements Book I, Propositions 1–5. Uses custom color scheme: postulates (red), common notions (purple), propositions (teal).

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
